article_title: EU Council Backs Delayed AI Act Enforcement as Civil Society Warns of Regulatory Retreat

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: EU Council Backs Delayed AI Act Enforcement as Civil Society Warns of Regulatory Retreat
content_preview.summary: The EU Council agreed to delay high-risk AI system rules to late 2027 and 2028, adding a deepfake ban while drawing civil society warnings of deregulation disguised as simplification.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
The Council of the European Union on March 13 adopted its negotiating mandate on a proposal to streamline the bloc's landmark AI Act, approving a package of amendments that would [delay enforcement of high-risk AI system rules](https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2026/03/13/council-agrees-position-to-streamline-rules-on-artificial-intelligence/) by more than a year while extending regulatory relief to a wider range of companies.

Under the Council's agreed position, standalon...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive policy article with excellent structure covering the Council decision, new prohibitions, SME relief, political divisions, and next steps. Well-balanced between pro-reform and civil society perspectives.
editor_notes.source_verification: Council URL (consilium.europa.eu) returned 403 but is an official EU institution source — the facts it supports are corroborated by the European Parliament legislative train page. European Parliament source confirms December 2027/August 2028 deadlines, political party divisions (EPP/Renew/ECR welcome, S&D/Greens/Left oppose), small mid-cap extensions, strict necessity standard, and US geopolitical pressure concerns. Euronews confirms BEUC criticism, Finance Watch warnings, November 2025 Digital Omnibus unveiling, and deregulation framing. The '127 civil society organizations' coalition claim is NOT confirmed by any of the three cited sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts verified. One unsourced claim: the coalition of '127 civil society organizations, trade unions, and public interest groups' is not found in any of the three cited sources. This may be accurate but lacks citation support.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and balanced — presents both institutional and civil society perspectives without taking sides.
editor_notes.originality: No existing article on the March 13 EU Council AI Act decision.
concerns: The '127 civil society organizations' claim is not supported by any of the three cited sources
recommendations: Source or soften the 127-organization coalition claim
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong policy journalism with good sourcing and balance. One minor unsourced detail. Approved.
